claims.claim: Excited about zero-knowledge / programmable cryptography as a durable foundational primitive for scaling and privacy
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about AI x crypto / decentralized compute (DePIN) as the most interesting new frontier
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about modular scaling / L2 infrastructure (rollups, cheaper data availability)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about account abstraction / wallet UX — smart accounts, key management, gas abstraction, seed-phrase-free onboarding
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Concerned about the US regulatory environment (enforcement-first ambiguity pushing builders offshore, structural overhang)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about speculative froth distracting builders and feeding bad-faith narratives about crypto's purpose
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Neutral risk regime — long-horizon infrastructure investor who funds through cycles, constructive on tech but mindful of froth, not timing the market
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Sharp, sustained anti-memecoin crusade — went well beyond generic 'froth,' repeatedly calling memecoins a 'casino,' 'net negative,' technically uninteresting, and damaging to adoption/regulation (Apr 24)
Onchain/crypto gaming as a sector of genuine interest — EVE Frontier playtests, ProofOfPlay L3 throughput, full gaming podcast (not anticipated in prediction)
Bullish crypto-payments thesis: 'Payments has been one of the goals of crypto from the beginning. The pieces are finally coming together' (Apr 25)
Enthusiasm for DeFi protocol design specifically — DELV fixed/variable-rate AMM, Morpho, Maker's two-token design (prediction was infra-only)
Extensive tokenomics / progressive-decentralization / network-token-design framework and DUNA legal structure — a major new emphasis despite prediction's 'I don't make token calls'
Deep focus on DAO governance and governance attacks (Compound incident analysis, voting mechanism design, Cicada private voting)
AI open-source / open-weights advocacy and safety position — argued GPT-class and uncensored models cause no identifiable harm and closed source limits innovation (Jul 23)
Open-source-as-public-good mission framing ('we will never monetize') around Jolt, Helios, Halmos
Interest in PoS slashing / permissionless-consensus security research and formal-verification tooling (Halmos)
notes: Window is Apr–Sep 2024. ZK is the single strongest hit: the Jolt Alpha zkVM launch and months of follow-up (Binius, RISC Zero/SP1 praise, Cicada, 'verifiable computing in zero knowledge') decisively confirm it. L2/modular scaling confirmed via the Optimism/superchain podcast, ProofOfPlay L3, and the Sep 30 endorsement of raising Ethereum's blob target. AI x crypto confirmed mainly via the endorsed 'AI agents can't have bank accounts, but they can have crypto wallets' quote plus heavy AI engagement — but note the prediction's specific DePIN/decentralized-compute/GPU-market framing was never directly addressed, so this is a stance-level rather than literal confirmation. Account abstraction is absent: no discussion of smart accounts, gas abstraction, or seed-phrase onboarding; the Helios-in-wallets mention is about light-client verification, a different thesis. Both concerns confirmed — regulatory concern via 'politicized regulators,' SEC/securities legal-risk framing, and progressive-decentralization-to-reduce-risk; froth via the anti-memecoin thread. Risk regime confirmed in spirit: he never makes directional/price calls, instead embodying a build-through-cycles posture (long lockups, public goods, 'real products will develop in the end'). Token buckets were intentionally empty in the prediction; consistent with that, he made no individual token price calls in the window (project praise stayed at the protocol/infra level), so there were no token claims to label.
